claim: Every docs/gen/ projection generated by plain `hotam gen-spec` (implemented_by TRACEABILITY.md/COVERAGE.md/REPO-MAP.md's own Build* functions) MUST render byte-identically regardless of whether the SAME `gen-spec` invocation also passed `--spec`, EXCEPT SPEC.md itself, which ALWAYS remains the sole `--spec`-shaped artifact whose staleness is separately enforced by check_spec_md_current.
evidence: task #317 CI fix chain: CI's gen-spec idempotency step failed because domains/hotam-spec-self/docs/gen/TRACEABILITY.md had been committed in --spec-shaped form (narration-verdict suffixes), but hotam land's own routine regeneration (cmd/hotam/land.go lines ~164/392/513) ALWAYS calls plain genSpec, never --spec -- so the committed --spec-shaped docs were inherently unstable, silently reverted by the very next hotam land

diagnosed by an independent design-review agent (@fm) as a genuine bug, not a CI-config mismatch: the fix made BuildTraceability/BuildCoverage pure, mode-independent functions of the graph plus a cheap AST scan (dropping their verdicts ...map[string]ScenarioVerdict variadic parameter entirely), and made REPO-MAP.md's SPEC.md listing stat-based (reads real on-disk content when present) rather than write-set-based

TestGenSpec_SharedProjectionsModeIndependent (cmd/hotam/gen_spec_test.go) mechanically proves genSpec(includeSpec=true) then genSpec(includeSpec=false) against the same unchanged graph leaves TRACEABILITY.md/COVERAGE.md/REPO-MAP.md byte-identical across both runs
why: hotam land's own routine regeneration always calls plain gen-spec (never --spec), so any docs/gen/ projection whose rendered bytes depend on --spec mode is structurally unstable the moment it is committed in --spec-shaped form: the next routine hotam land silently reverts it, and CI's gen-spec idempotency step (which also runs plain gen-spec) then disagrees with whatever mode last regenerated the committed baseline locally -- exactly what broke CI in task #317. Anchoring this property to the graph, enforced_by the new regression test, closes the class of bug rather than only the one instance: any FUTURE docs/gen/ projection that wants to render a real, --spec-only executed signal must either (a) also render byte-identically regardless of mode like TRACEABILITY.md/COVERAGE.md now do, or (b) become a second SPEC.md-shaped exception with its own explicit staleness gate -- never a silent third mode-dependent file that only gen-spec --spec regenerates correctly. Left as DRAFT enforced_by (no check_* wired in yet) pending human review per R-decided-needs-human-signoff / R-ai-presents-not-decides -- this file documents the proposal but is NOT applied to the graph by the same task that authored it.
